tasks.title: Add versioning support for scan prompts (v1/v2) with v2 as default
evidence: Current scan prompts are hardcoded in scan.rs as MAINTENANCE_MODE_GUIDANCE and INNOVATION_MODE_GUIDANCE constants (v1)
User has v2 prompts ready at /tmp/scan-maintenance-v2.md and /tmp/scan-innovation-v2.md
Need config-controlled version selection with v2 as the new default
V2 prompts need alterations for JSON schema requirements and project type/user focus injection points
plan: Add ScanPromptVersion enum (v1, v2) and scan_prompt_version field to AgentConfig in contracts/config.rs
Add merge_from support for the new config field
Create versioned prompt asset files: scan_maintenance_v1.md, scan_maintenance_v2.md, scan_innovation_v1.md, scan_innovation_v2.md
Extract existing v1 content from scan.rs constants to scan_*_v1.md files
Process v2 prompts from /tmp/ paths: add JSON schema requirements for queue tasks, add {{PROJECT_TYPE_GUIDANCE}} and {{USER_FOCUS}} injection points
Update scan.md template to support versioned {{MODE_GUIDANCE}} substitution
Update scan.rs render_scan_prompt() to load versioned prompts based on config.scan_prompt_version
Update registry.rs to register new prompt template IDs for versioned scan prompts
Run make generate to update schemas/config.schema.json with new field
Add tests for versioned prompt loading and rendering
Run make ci to verify all checks pass
tasks.request: Let's add a versioning tag for the 'scan' prompts for maintenance mode and innovation mode. The current prompts we can label 'v1' and we control which prompt versions are used based on a default config variable and a key in the config json schema. I have the v2 prompts ready and they are in the files at the paths below. These will be 'v2'. I want v2 to be the new default. Maintenance v2: /tmp/scan-maintenance-v2.md, /tmp/scan-innovation-v2.md. The prompts in these paths will need some slight alterations to ENSURE the scan agents are provided the proper JSON schema requirements for adding to the queue, and to allow the project type injection, and user focus injection, and the mandate of at least 10 new tasks with no upper limit.

tasks.title: Implement subtask support for hierarchical task breakdown
evidence: No parent/child relationship fields in Task schema
Large tasks cannot be broken down into trackable subtasks
PRD multi-task mode creates sequential dependencies but not true subtasks
plan: Add `parent_id` field to Task for child-to-parent reference
Add `subtasks` array field or compute dynamically from parent_id
Add `ralph task subtask create <PARENT_ID> <REQUEST>` command
Update parent task progress based on subtask completion percentage
Block parent task completion until all subtasks are done
Update TUI to show collapsible subtask hierarchies
Update queue graph to show parent-child relationships
Add tests for subtask lifecycle and parent completion blocking

tasks.title: Add conditional task execution based on custom conditions
evidence: No conditional execution support in task schema
Cannot skip tasks based on file existence, env vars, or command output
All tasks in queue are attempted regardless of context
plan: Add `condition` field to Task with type and parameters
Support condition types: file_exists, env_set, command_success, task_status
Add `ralph task condition <TASK_ID> <TYPE> <PARAM>` command
Evaluate condition before task execution in run one/loop
Skip task with log message if condition not met
Add `--skip-conditions` flag to bypass condition checks
Add tests for each condition type

tasks.title: Implement IDE extensions for VS Code and JetBrains
evidence: No IDE extensions exist for Ralph
Users must switch between terminal and editor to check task status
No inline task creation from TODO comments in code
plan: Create VS Code extension with task tree view
Show current task, queue, and done tasks in sidebar
Add commands: run task, mark done, create task from selection
Highlight TODO/FIXME comments with quick-create task actions
Create JetBrains plugin with equivalent functionality
Support task status indicators in editor gutter
Add configuration for ralph binary path
Document installation and usage for both extensions
